reasoning: Relapsing fever, caused by various Borrelia species, has no approved vaccine for human use as of the knowledge cutoff (2025-11-09). Development is hindered by the bacteria's extreme antigenic variation. Recent medical updates from authoritative sources (StatPearls, May 2025; CDC, January 2025; Medscape, December 2024) do not mention any human vaccine trial progressing toward approval. The Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' was confirmed to list the status as 'No' as recently as October 30, 2025. Given the complexity of vaccine development and the regulatory timeline, it is extremely unlikely that a vaccine would be developed and recognized on Wikipedia by the resolution dates in late 2025 or early 2026. The probabilities for later dates increase slightly to reflect greater uncertainty over longer horizons, but remain low due to the lack of any clinical pipeline candidates.
